prompt: A cracked, scorched wooden kitchen table photographed from directly overhead, filling the entire frame. Burned into the wood is a faintly visible world map — its geography unmistakable, continents readable. The Middle East, Gaza, the West Bank, Ukraine, Sudan, and Yemen glow with deep orange-red ember light from beneath the wood, as if fire lives inside those regions. The rest of the map is cold, dark, and ash-grey. Scattered across the glowing hotspot regions are hundreds of tiny handmade cloth dolls — simple, faceless, the kind a child would own — most of them burned, blackened, or collapsed. A few dozen pristine, colorful, untouched dolls sit on the cold grey regions of the map. The sheer number of burned dolls over the Middle East and Africa versus the intact dolls elsewhere makes the imbalance impossible to miss. At the very center of the Gaza region, a single white candle burns as the only active light source, casting long shadows across the fallen dolls. One overturned child's cup sits at the table's edge. No flags. No weapons. No soldiers. No text. No labels. No explosions. Hyperrealistic, top-down Phase One camera perspective, 8K, extremely detailed fabric and wood textures, ash and ember color palette, the pristine dolls are small, vivid, and colorful by contrast. The emotional register is witness, not spectacle.
dataset_name: ACLED Armed Conflict Location and Event Data — Global Conflict Fatalities 2023–2025
insight: In 2024–2025, civilian casualties in active conflict zones — Gaza, Ukraine, Sudan, Yemen — are overwhelmingly concentrated in the Global South and Middle East, while most of the Western world remains physically untouched by direct violence.
